external.hook: Amid global crude supply disruptions, Singapore's role as a primary refined fuel supplier is being tested as international partners rely on bilateral ties to secure critical energy imports, highlighting Singapore's strategic position in the regional energy supply chain.
external.title: Fuel crisis: The old friendship that could keep New Zealand’s fuel flowing

external.hook: Singapore's significant dependence on Qatari LNG and its limited storage capacity create a high-stakes operational vulnerability to spiking spot prices and supply shocks caused by the ongoing regional energy crisis.
external.title: How Asian countries are shielding their economies from soaring oil prices

external.hook: Escalating tensions in the Middle East are driving up the cost of a crucial plastics ingredient used in medical products, creating immediate operational and cost pressures for global healthcare supply chains, including those serving Singapore.
external.title: Middle East tensions push up cost of key plastics ingredient used in medical supplies

internal.hook: Ports in Singapore see increasing congestion and delays as shipping routes are diverted due to conflict.
internal.title: Shipping disruption from Middle East crisis reaches ports in Asia, including Singapore

internal.hook: Analysis of how Singapore's fuel bunkering industry is managing logistical hurdles and energy supply chain shocks.
internal.title: In-depth: Singapore bunkering sector clarifies uncertainty, copes with supply disruptions

internal.hook: Singapore to assist in fuel supply security following logistics challenges in Australia.
internal.title: Australia inks agreement with Singapore for fuel supply as pumps run dry
